entities.name: sigil

attributes.instruction: Engrave name of angel and wind

entities.raw_quote: Engrave on the sigil the name of the angel and the wind serving the Moon

summary: This chunk discusses the historical influences on the Liber Juratus Honorii, specifically Western/Latin and Byzantine Christianity. It details various angelic hierarchies, planetary spirits (particularly for the Moon), and compares name variations across different manuscripts like the Heptameron and Astromagia of Alfonso X.
key_passages: Honorius includes both the Apostle’s Creed and the Athanasian Creed
Engrave on the sigil the name of the angel and the wind serving the Moon, and commanding over its sign (which is Cancer), with his aides.
Each group of air demons and wind demons is under the authority of three angels who, under the direction of an archangel, impose his authority through a special seal.
The concept of a great seal, commonly called the Seal of Solomon, is central to Solomonic magic.
unusual variation of the Tetragrammaton, “Ioth He Vau Deleth,” found in two places in the text
